name: The Kubernetes Citadel
description: A towering fortress of light and logic stands at the edge of the digital frontier. Within its walls, containers march in formation, orchestrated by an unseen intelligence. You are a new Systems Architect — your mission: master the Citadel before the chaos of unscheduled pods consumes it.

lore.worldOrigin: The Kubernetes Citadel was forged in the era of the Great Containerization, when engineers discovered that individual processes — lone soldiers — could not survive the battles of scale. They were grouped into Pods, organized into Deployments, and connected through Services. The Citadel is their home — and your training ground.
lore.coreConflict: Unscheduled and crashed pods have broken free of the scheduler's control. They roam the cluster, consuming resources and corrupting node assignments. As a new Architect, you must reclaim each sector of the Citadel by mastering the Kubernetes primitives that govern it.

stages.name: The Pod Barracks
stages.description: The foundational housing units of the Citadel. Every container lives inside a Pod. Master the Pod and you master the atom of Kubernetes.

boss.name: The Pending Pod
boss.title: Specter of the Unscheduled
boss.description: A pod trapped in perpetual limbo — neither running nor terminated, consuming a scheduler slot and blocking progress. Its health regenerates as long as the cluster has insufficient resources.
boss.defeatCondition: Answer all concept checks about Pod anatomy and scheduling

stages.name: The Service Gateway
stages.description: The Citadel's communication layer. Without Services, Pods are islands — powerful, but isolated. The Gateway connects them to the outside world and to each other.

boss.name: The CrashLoop Specter
boss.title: Echo of the Broken Endpoint
boss.description: A Service that points to no living Pod — its endpoint list eternally empty, its selector mismatched. It haunts the Gateway, absorbing traffic and returning nothing but timeouts.
boss.defeatCondition: Successfully configure a ClusterIP and NodePort service

stages.name: The Deployment Citadel Core
stages.description: The heart of the Citadel. Deployments are the Orchestration Order's greatest achievement — self-healing, self-scaling units of war. Conquer this sector and the Citadel bends to your will.

boss.name: The Deployment Daemon
boss.title: Corruptor of ReplicaSets
boss.description: A rogue Deployment controller that spawns ReplicaSets endlessly, never cleaning up old revisions. It fills etcd with ghost entries and causes rollouts that never converge.
boss.defeatCondition: Perform a successful rolling update and rollback

stages.name: The ConfigMap Vault
stages.description: A sealed vault where environment configurations are stored. Breaching the ConfigMap Corruptor's hold here means no application config can be trusted — but mastering this stage gives you control over all runtime behavior.

boss.name: The ConfigMap Corruptor
boss.title: Poisoner of Environment Variables
boss.description: A malevolent entity that intercepts ConfigMap mounts and env injections, replacing values with corrupted data. Applications that rely on its poisoned configs crash silently — no error, just wrong behavior.
boss.defeatCondition: Correctly inject a ConfigMap as both an env variable and a mounted volume

stages.name: The Ingress Battlements
stages.description: The outer walls of the Citadel — the boundary between the internal cluster network and the chaotic external internet. Whoever controls the Ingress controls who enters and who is turned away.

boss.name: The Ingress Interceptor
boss.title: Guardian of Misrouted Paths
boss.description: A corrupted Ingress controller that captures all inbound traffic and routes it to a 404 void. It rewrites host headers, drops path prefixes, and intercepts TLS termination — leaving all external traffic stranded at the gate.
boss.defeatCondition: Configure an Ingress with host-based routing and at least two path rules

npcs.name: KubeWise
npcs.title: Elder Architect of the Orchestration Order
npcs.description: An ancient systems architect who has survived every major Kubernetes release since 1.0. KubeWise speaks in deprecation warnings and release notes, but his wisdom is hard-won and always accurate.
npcs.introDialogue: Ah, a new recruit. The Citadel has been waiting for you. Listen carefully — in this cluster, understanding comes before action. A reckless kubectl apply has toppled more architectures than any enemy.
hintDialogues: Remember: a Pod without resource limits is a ticking time bomb. The scheduler cannot plan around the unknown.
Services use label selectors, not names. If your selector does not match your Pod labels, the endpoint list will be empty — and the Specter feeds on empty endpoints.
A Deployment's power is in its declarative nature. Tell it the desired state. Let it figure out how to get there.

npcs.name: CTRL
npcs.title: Automated Navigator of the Citadel
npcs.description: A compact AI construct who acts as the player's in-world terminal. CTRL speaks in short, precise sentences and always includes the relevant kubectl command. Think of CTRL as a very smart, slightly sarcastic autocomplete.
npcs.introDialogue: Online. Designation: CTRL. I navigate. You execute. Let's begin with `kubectl get pods`. Go.
hintDialogues: Stuck? Try `kubectl describe pod <name>`. The Events section at the bottom tells you everything.
`kubectl logs <pod>` — because guessing is not debugging.
Use `-n <namespace>` or you'll spend an hour wondering why nothing shows up. You're welcome.

enemies.name: Evicted Pod Wraith
enemies.description: The ghost of a Pod that was evicted due to resource pressure. It drifts through the cluster, consuming small amounts of CPU on the nodes it visits — a reminder of the cost of under-specifying resource limits.

enemies.name: OOMKill Shade
enemies.description: Manifests when a container exceeds its memory limit and is terminated by the kernel. The Shade strikes fast and without warning, dealing spike damage — modeling the unpredictable nature of out-of-memory kills.

enemies.name: Stale ReplicaSet Specter
enemies.description: An old ReplicaSet left behind by a Deployment rollout, no longer managing any pods but still occupying etcd space. It slows the API server subtly and spawns confusion for Architects who don't understand revision history.

completionReward.title: Citadel Commander
completionReward.description: You have mastered the five pillars of the Kubernetes Citadel. The Orchestration Order has granted you the rank of Citadel Commander. The cluster is yours.
